{{Quote|They always lock the door…you think they'd have learned by now.|[[Kyle Katarn]], when he and [[Jaden Korr]] tried to enter Bast Castle|audio=AlwaysLockTheDoor.flac|<ref name="Jedi Academy" />}}
'''Bast Castle''' was the private fortress of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]—[[Dark Lord of the Sith]] and [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Forces]]—located on the [[Acid rain/Legends|acid rain-soaked]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]]. Long after his demise, it became the headquarters of the [[Dark Side Elite]] and its leaders, the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Sedriss QL]] and later [[Xecr Nist]].

==History==
{{Quote|There it is, X2... Bast Castle! It resonates with the power of the Dark Side. Even here, I can still sense my father's anger and regret…|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to [[X2]] at the [[Battle of Vjun]]|<ref name="Elite Squadron" />}}
The secret sanctuary of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Forces]]' [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|secondary]] [[Commander-in-Chief/Legends|commander-in-chief]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], Bast [[Castle/Legends|Castle]] was an immense [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Fortress/Legends|fortress]] located near a [[Beach/Legends|beach]] on [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]]. [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Vader often retired to Bast, his personal home, when not on assignment for the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]]. Very few [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] knew of its existence and it was here in solitude that he studied the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and attempted to learn what [[Gender/Legends|he]] needed to heal his broken body, frequently using his personal [[Meditation chamber/Legends|meditation chamber]] located at the top of the bastion. When not delving into the dark side, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] spent what time he had perfecting his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] skills, using several variants of [[Training droid/Legends|training droids]] to challenge himself. The castle also contained a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] workshop and private [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] where Vader would use his [[Technician/Legends|mechanical]] skills he picked from his time as a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] to privately work on his [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced X1]] and to repair his sparring droids. Vader also [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] a [[Unidentified Toydarian subject|Toydarian]] to [[Death/Legends|death]] there once.<ref name="The Essential Guide to Alien Species real-world book">[[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]</ref><ref name="Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force real-life book">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> Aside from the Dark Lord himself, the castle was occupied by a [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] who both stationed within the castle and the surrounding outposts. These [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] were assigned to protect Bast Castle while Vader was away to fulfill his commission or on another assignment for [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. The citadel was not the only redoubt at the disposal of the Dark Lord, with a public [[Darth Vader's Palace|palace]] awaiting him on [[Coruscant/Legends|Imperial Center]], also known as Coruscant, near the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Emperor's own]]. After the [[Death/Legends|death]] of both sovereigns, the fortress stood alone and forgotten for [[Standard year/Legends|years]].

After the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the fallen [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] [[X1]] decided to forge a new empire with himself on the throne. Declaring himself a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], he sought to attain the power of the dark side. However, he lacked the knowledge of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and thus traveled to the Vjun in order to acquire a [[X1's holocron|holocron]] located at Bast Castle. His brother [[X2]] traveled to the fortress as well in order to stop him, but by the time he reached the surface he learned that his [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] brother had departed for the [[Volcano/Legends|volcanic]] world of [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]].<ref name="Elite Squadron" />

Prior to [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] resurgence in yet another clone body, Bast Castle was established as the headquarters of the [[Dark Side Elite]] by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Sedriss QL]], whose role was built as a lesser substitute of the late Lord Vader. To honor the fortress' previous resident, Sedriss had a statue of Vader erected. Following Palpatine's return, the reborn Emperor interrogated and tortured inexperienced [[Ysanna/Legends|Ysanna]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in Bast Castle under the gaze of a massive statue of his old apprentice. When [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Kam Solusar]] attacked the citadel to rescue the Ysanna, it was heavily damaged in the fighting, and Vader's statue was toppled, crushing several of the Dark Jedi beneath it.<ref name="EE 1">[[Empire's End 1|''Empire's End'' 1]]</ref>

In [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Jaden Korr]] visited Vjun and the castle in order to try and stop the [[Disciples of Ragnos]] from draining it of its [[The Force/Legends|Force]] energy. It was then that [[Rosh Penin]] revealed that he had turned to the dark side. Prior to the encounter with Penin, Korr came across the toppled statue of Vader and jokingly told himself that somebody really hadn't liked the [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]. Korr eventually defeated Penin and killed the [[Kothos twins]] who accompanied Penin and empowered him with the Force.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

[[File:AnakinSoloVsOrloc-KenobisBlade.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Anakin Solo attacking Orloc with Obi-Wan Kenobi's lightsaber.]]
Later, in [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], the [[Jedi historian]] [[Tionne Solusar]] discovered that Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] had been recovered by the Sith Lord Darth Vader and stored at Bast Castle. Since an information broker had sold this information to them, she feared that others may have gotten to it first. Thinking it was just a simple trip and time for training, Tionne allowed [[Anakin Solo]], [[Tahiri Veila]], [[Uldir Lochett]], and [[Ikrit]] to accompany her. They left on her [[Jemlaat-class in-system sail yacht|''Jemlaat''-class yacht]] ''[[Lore Seeker]]''.

Having arrived at Bast Castle, they discovered another vessel and feared another group had reached it first. Having survived the fortress's automated defense systems and booby traps, they discovered the lightsaber in a secret alcove. Unfortunately, a cloaked man named [[Orloc]] arrived. Orloc stole Kenobi's lightsaber and claimed to be a mage who possessed great powers.

In response, the Jedi pursued Orloc and his henchmen on a chase through Bast Castle. They managed to defeat Orloc and escape Vader's old fortress, along with Kenobi's lightsaber and the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] of the ancient Jedi Master [[Asli Krimsan]].

==The statue of Darth Vader==
{{Quote|Someone didn't like Vader much…|[[Jaden Korr]] upon seeing the destroyed statue|audio=SomeoneDidntLikeVaderMuch-JediAcademy.flac|<ref name="Jedi Academy" />}}
[[File:Vader_statue_at_Bast_castle.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Vader's destroyed statue in Bast Castle.]]
After the death of its original owner, Bast Castle became the stronghold of the Emperor's Dark Jedi. As a homage to the Supreme Commander of Imperial Forces and Dark Lord of the Sith in whose shoes he had stepped as the new [[Executor (Imperial rank)|Military Executor]], Sedriss QL, erected a statue of Darth Vader in the middle of Bast Castle.<ref name="DE II 6" /> Carved from black stone, it stood about 6 meters tall. Palpatine demanded that the image of his treacherous [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] to be destroyed, but in an act of defiance, Sedriss overlooked the order because of his admiration for Vader's abilities.

Sedriss was eventually killed by [[Ood Bnar]]<ref name="DE II 3">[[Dark Empire II 3|''Dark Empire II'' 3]]</ref> and the ownership of the castle fell to the new Executor, [[Xecr Nist]].<ref name="EE 1" /> Palpatine ordered once again that the statue be removed,<ref name="DE II 6" /> and wishing to avoid the fate of his predecessor, Nist promised that he would, though he similarly dared not tear down the idol of Darth Vader. However, before the deed could ever be done, a task force of Jedi including Luke Skywalker, [[Rayf Ysanna]], Kam Solusar, and [[Empatojayos Brand]] attacked the stronghold. A vicious fight ensued between the four Jedi and the Dark Side Elite.<ref name="EE 1" />

In an ironic twist, Skywalker defeated Nist and used the Force to push the enormous statue upon the remaining Dark Side Elite, killing them and winning the day for the Jedi.<ref name="EE 1" /> The statue lay broken in Bast Castle for years and the rubble was still present when Kyle Katarn and his student Jaden Korr visited the planet in an attempt to stop the [[Disciples of Ragnos]] from siphoning the Force energy.<ref name="Jedi Academy" /> Anakin Solo and Tahiri also visited the castle during their training at the [[Jedi Praxeum]] and witnessed the remnants of the massive statue.

==Defenses and garrison==
[[File:JKAlevelshot_vjun2.png|thumb|left|220px|The [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] located inside Bast Castle]]
Bast Castle was surrounded with a complex network of manned defenses that extended several kilometers into the surrounding ravines. It was also equipped with at least one [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turret. The facility also contained a number of [[ASP-38 Droid]]s that were tasked with eliminating intruders though many of these were eliminated by X2.

During Jaden Korr and Kyle Katarn's [[Mission to Vjun (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|mission to Vjun]], a pair of [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bombers]] strafed a valley and entrance to the Castle. One starfighter hangar was located on the upper levels, housing two inactive [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter|TIE/LN starfighters]] and Vader's own TIE Advanced x1. Later in their quest they encountered several locked doors that proved impervious to [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]. The two Jedi passed through the Castle's extensive shield generation facility, moving through several control rooms and causing the collapse of the power column room through which gap they were able to gain access to the Upper Castle.

The Castle appeared to have an extensive garrison of stormtroopers, many equipped as [[Jumptrooper/Legends|rocket troopers]] or [[hazard stormtrooper]]s, in addition to many [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web]] Emplacements. From ruins found by Jaden Korr, [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]] were most likely stationed at Bast Castle at some point in time.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:VadersCastleConcept.jpg|thumb|right|Concept art of Vader's castle for ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]
Early concept art for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] included a sketch by [[Ralph McQuarrie]] depicting what was to be Vader's castle.<ref name="Magic of Myth">Concept art for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] seen at [[Star Wars: The Magic of Myth]], now available through the official [http://www.nasm.si.edu/exhibitions/starwars/sw-unit8.htm National Air and Space Museum website]. Also seen at [[Star Wars: The Exhibition]].</ref>
